beats.text: You made bar graphs with scale of 10. You read values at the lines and between the lines. Let's see what you know.

incorrects.description: Student selected 4: counted marks instead of reading the scale value

steps.text: You counted the marks. Look at the NUMBER where the bar ends. What number is at the top of Fiction?

incorrects.description: Student selected 35: estimated between 30 and 40 when bar ends at axis line

params.description: Horizontal guideline draws from Fiction bar to axis.

steps.text: The bar ends right at a line. Follow it to the axis. Which number is right there?

incorrects.description: Student selected 45: overshot the actual bar height

params.description: Horizontal guideline draws from Fiction bar to axis at 40.

steps.text: Follow the top of the bar straight across to the axis. Where does it meet the numbers?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Horizontal line draws from top of Fiction bar to axis at 40, which highlights.

steps.text: Let me show you. I look at where the Fiction bar ends. I follow that line across to the axis numbers. It meets the axis right at 40. The Fiction bar shows 40.

incorrects.description: Student selected 30, the lower boundary line instead of the midpoint

steps.text: The bar doesn't stop at 30. Look where the top of the bar ends. It's higher than 30, between 30 and 40.

incorrects.description: Student selected 34, close to correct but not the midpoint

steps.text: The bar ends exactly halfway between 30 and 40. Half of 10 is 5. Count from 30: that's 35, not 34.

incorrects.description: Student selected 40, the upper boundary line instead of the midpoint

steps.text: The bar doesn't reach 40. Look at where the top ends. It's between 30 and 40, right in the middle.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Horizontal guideline draws from top of Team A bar to vertical axis, stopping at 35.

steps.text: Let me show you. The bar ends halfway between 30 and 40. Each interval is 10, so half of 10 is 5. I count from 30: 30 plus 5 equals 35. The bar shows 35 because it ends at the midpoint between those two lines.

incorrects.description: Student did not correctly place all four bars on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Check the data table values and where each bar should end.

incorrects.description: Student did not correctly place all four bars on second attempt

params.description: Markers and Paintbrushes highlight on the axis, showing they should align with lines at 30 and 20.

steps.text: Markers is 30, right at a line. Paintbrushes is 20, also at a line. Crayons is 45, halfway between 40 and 50. Glue Sticks is 55, halfway between 50 and 60. Where does each bar end?

incorrects.description: Student did not correctly place all four bars. System models the answer.

params.description: System sets all four bars: Markers at 30, Crayons at 45, Paintbrushes at 20, Glue Sticks at 55.

steps.text: Let me show you. Markers is 30, right at the 30 line. Crayons is 45, halfway between 40 and 50. Paintbrushes is 20, at the 20 line. Glue Sticks is 55, halfway between 50 and 60. Some bars end at the lines. Some end between the lines. The correct graph shows all four values from the data table.

incorrects.description: Student selected 55 (Glue Sticks total instead of difference)

params.description: Glue Sticks and Markers bars highlight.

steps.text: You found Glue Sticks. But the question asks how many MORE. Find the difference between Glue Sticks and Markers.

incorrects.description: Student selected 85 (added 55 + 30 instead of finding difference)

params.description: Glue Sticks and Markers bars highlight.

steps.text: You added the two bars. When we ask how many MORE, we subtract. What is 55 minus 30?

incorrects.description: Student selected 15 (possibly confused with another comparison or miscalculated)

params.description: Glue Sticks and Markers bars highlight.

steps.text: Check the heights. Glue Sticks is at 55, Markers is at 30. How far apart are they?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Visual indicator draws showing difference between Glue Sticks (55) and Markers (30) bars.

steps.text: Here's how to find it. Glue Sticks is at 55. Markers is at 30. To find how many MORE, we subtract: 55 minus 30 equals 25. The answer is 25.

params.title: Art Supplies Collected
params.description: Vertical bar graph titled Art Supplies Collected appears. Four bars: Markers 30, Crayons 45, Paintbrushes 20, Glue Sticks 55. Scale of 10. Glue Sticks and Markers bars highlighted from prior comparison.

beats.text: You can read bar graphs with a scale of 10, even values between the lines. You're ready to practice.

params.description: Bar graph fades and clears from screen.
